_comment: WC2026 Round of 32, 2026-06-29, Boston Stadium. KNOCKOUT: no draw -- level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties, so the deliverable is who ADVANCES. Routes: Germany won Group E (7-1 Curacao, 2-1 Ivory Coast, 1-2 Ecuador; 6 pts, rating 88) -- strong attack but the 7-1 vs minnow Curacao inflates the goal record, and they LOST the dead rubber to Ecuador, so not flawless. Paraguay qualified as a BEST THIRD from Group D (1-4 USA, 1-0 Turkey, 0-0 Australia; 4 pts, GD -2, GF 2, rating 70) -- a disciplined, defensive, low-scoring side (two clean-ish games, only broke down vs USA). So this is the favourite-vs-organized-low-block archetype (England-Ghana / Canada-South Africa, but a bigger class gap): Germany the heavy favourite, but Paraguay sits deep and grinds, keeping it tight -> do NOT expect a rout despite the rating gap, and the tight game RAISES the ET/penalty share, lifting Paraguay's advancement above its 90' win. Full motivation, no rotation. Home = Germany (nominal; neutral venue). Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Single-elimination: level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties. Germany the heavy favourite by class; Paraguay a disciplined low block (0-0 Australia, 1-0 Turkey) that qualified as a best third -> expect a tight, low-scoring game, not a rout, with a real chance Paraguay drags it to extra time.

home.name: Germany
notes: Quality (rating 88); strong attack but the 7-1 vs Curacao inflates it and they lost 1-2 to Ecuador. attack 1.30.
Decent but leaked to Ecuador. defense 0.92.
Heavy favourite, but faces a packed block -> not a rout; tight game read (rho -0.08).

away.name: Paraguay
notes: Disciplined, low-scoring (0-0 Australia, 1-0 Turkey, GF 2 in the group). attack 0.78 -- limited going forward, but no over-downgrade.
Organized block, hard to break down. defense 0.95 -> keeps it tight.
Knockout: a tight game drags toward ET/penalties, lifting the underdog's advancement above its 90' win.
